danger_signals.visual_form: Basic enemy sprite with blue coloring
danger_signals.meaning: Standard threat; direct charge attack pattern signals immediate danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Ninja sprite with visible sword weapon
danger_signals.meaning: Elevated threat level; leap attack with melee range danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile sprites thrown by ninja enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Long-range danger; ranged attack threat from distance
Mobile threat; unpredictable movement patterns increase danger difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite with fire visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard combined with enemy threat; fire damage overlay

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct sprite with unique design (Karnov, Iron Arm, Kamui, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Stage culmination threat; significant difficulty spike signaling level completion gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Final unique boss sprite in helicopter setting
danger_signals.meaning: Critical world-state threat; kidnapper presence indicates presidential danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprite items scattered in levels

safety_signals.meaning: Temporal safety buffer; extended window for level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Landmark architectural imagery in ending scene
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone reached; mission objective location secured

faction_indicators.color_identity: Character 1: white pants | Character 2: green pants
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Street-smart brawler aesthetic; tough casual appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Downtown New York City streets as home territory; casual urban setting

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue (standard ninja enemies); varied for bosses
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ninja visual identity; traditional Japanese martial arts aesthetic; dragon association (leader name)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Blue basic ninjas | Sword-wielders | Shuriken-throwers | Kunoichi (female variant) | Fire-infused variant | Boss tier: Karnov, Iron Arm, Kamui, Animal, Akaikage, Devil Pole, Dragon Ninja
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban locations (NYC streets, sewers, forest, train, cave, underground factory); infiltration across American landscape; helicopter base (final headquarters)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: White House location; Secret Service agent liaison; President Ronnie figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Washington D.C. as capital/threat origin; White House as rescue destination; security guard presence (epilogue)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White House security breach; ninja infiltration across multiple American locations (NYC, sewers, forests, trains, caves)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dragon Ninja gang active across all level stages; escalating enemy density and boss tier encounters; time pressure mechanic reflects urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Introductory text: 'Rampant ninja related crimes these days... Whitehouse is not the exception'
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Widespread ninja enemy presence throughout urban and wilderness environments; normalized threat across diverse locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Epilogue: President Ronnie alive and celebrating with Bad Dudes at White House with security guards present (US version) OR receiving statue reward (Japan version)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies cease appearing; celebratory animation; safe location secured; normal government function restored

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential level environments showing progression through kidnapper escape route (NYC → Truck → Sewer → Forest → Train → Cave → Factory → Helicopter)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy encounters scale with level progression; boss encounters gate advancement; power-up availability fluctuates; time pressure remains constant

environmental_storytelling.example: Chase sequence visualized through level transitions: downtown NYC (crime scene) → moving truck (escape attempt) → underground sewers (hiding route) → forest (remote refuge) → freight train (transport method) → cave system (natural barrier) → underground factory (Dragon Ninja base) → helicopter (final confrontation). Each environment visually represents ninja evasion strategy.
Game introduction features dialogue from Secret Service agent establishing kidnapping as official government crisis, immediately communicating world-state threat level through exposition

Multiple ninja archetypes (basic, sword-wielders, projectile users, acrobatic females, fire-infused variants, plus specialized bosses) signal Dragon Ninja organization has hierarchical structure and diverse combat capabilities

Each stage boss (Karnov, Iron Arm, Kamui, Animal, Akaikage, Devil Pole, Dragon Ninja leader) has unique sprite design and special attack signature, establishing individual martial master identities within gang hierarchy
Limited weapon and health pickups scattered throughout levels indicate supply constraints; players must manage resources, visually communicating desperation of rescue mission
Countdown timer visible on screen creates visual tension; reflects urgency of presidential rescue and suggests immediate threat to hostage safety

US version: White House with security guards and President Ronnie celebrating restoration of governmental security. Japan version: Statue reward reflects cultural honor system. Both communicate successful restoration of political order

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Direct charge toward player; aggressive approach signals offensive threat
Leap attack pattern; verticality-based assault indicates higher skill tier
Projectile attacks from distance; behavioral spacing differs from melee units
Acrobatic movement patterns; unpredictable positioning creates evasion difficulty
Pack-based threat; lower individual threat but increases enemy formation density
Contact damage combined with attack pattern; visual fire effect communicates damage type
Fire-breathing special attack; ranged threat requiring evasion and positioning strategy
Illusion creation (self-copying mechanic); visual duplication confuses player targeting
Helicopter setting indicates escape attempt; final mechanical behavior (unspecified but implies climactic difficulty)
Immobile hostage; absence drives mission motivation
Celebration behavior; actively present celebrating with Bad Dudes, indicating safety restoration
Dialogue delivery; static briefing role communicates authority and mission legitimacy

notes: Bad Dudes uses straightforward visual communication: enemy color/type variations directly indicate threat level, progression through recognizable American locations grounds political narrative, and boss encounters provide clear difficulty gates. The game relies on sprite variation and environmental progression to convey world-state rather than subtle visual design. Political elements (presidential kidnapping, Secret Service, government restoration) are explicitly stated in dialogue rather than visually implied. Faction identity is communicated through aesthetic (ninja vs. street-smart American brawlers) rather than symbolic complexity.
